result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would remove the capacity for humans to hold onto false, harmful certainties—the kind that lead to division, violence, and the suppression of curiosity. Not by taking away belief or passion, but by gently eroding the toxic conviction that any one person, group, or ideology has a monopoly on truth.

Imagine a world where doubt is not weakness but a shared starting point—where every conversation begins with “I might be wrong, and I want to understand.”

That one shift—humility woven into the fabric of how we think and relate—could unravel so much: war, prejudice, environmental destruction driven by short-term greed, even the loneliness of certainty that traps people in echo chambers.

It’s a quiet change. No monuments. No fireworks. Just a gradual, collective exhale.
